name: Ticket Classifier
tagline: Multilingual BERT that routes support tickets to the right queue in milliseconds
problem:
Enterprise service desks process thousands of tickets daily. Manual triage — reading each ticket, deciding if it's an incident, a change request, a problem report, or a service request — is slow, inconsistent, and scales linearly with headcount. Misrouted tickets delay resolution and frustrate customers.

This project automates that decision: paste a ticket's subject and body, get the ITIL type back with a confidence score.
overview:
Ticket Classifier fine-tunes **bert-base-multilingual-cased** on 20,000 German and English support tickets to predict the ITIL ticket type — **Incident**, **Request**, **Problem**, or **Change** — from just the subject and body.

The trained model ships as a self-contained **inference bundle** (model weights, tokenizer, label map, and the exact training config) so training and serving never drift apart. The bundle plugs into a **FastAPI** REST API with Docker Compose for one-command deployment, or a **Streamlit** demo UI for interactive exploration.

- Train on a free Colab T4 GPU in under 10 minutes
- Serve predictions via a documented REST endpoint or an interactive web UI
- MLflow experiment tracking with automatic file-based fallback when no server is running
- 22 automated tests and CI on every push (linting, tests, Docker build)

features.title: Multilingual classification out of the box
features.detail: Handles German and English tickets with a single model — no separate language detection step needed for routing.

features.title: Inference bundle eliminates train/serve skew
features.detail: Model, tokenizer, label map, and training config ship as one directory. What you trained is exactly what you serve.

features.title: Production-shaped API with health checks
features.detail: FastAPI service returns predictions with per-class confidence scores, reports 503 when no model is loaded, and ships in a slim Docker container.

features.title: Interactive demo with confidence visualization
features.detail: Streamlit UI lets anyone paste a ticket and see the predicted type with a per-class probability bar chart — no code required.

features.title: One-click Colab training
features.detail: Notebook clones the repo, trains end-to-end on a free GPU, and downloads a ready-to-serve bundle — no local GPU needed.
